product_title: Allegra K Women Long Sleeves Scoop Neck Mesh Panel Top L Black
clean_review_title: Comparison of L vs. XL top - for those who wear a 12, 14 or 16 top!
clean_review_text: Having received conflicting advice/opinions on whether size L or XL would fit me best, I opted to purchase both sizes, as I am a Prime member, and could purchase this top for less than $13 with free returns. For reference, I am 5' 4 1/2" tall, with a 38DD/40D bust (depends on brand), a 35" waist and 45" hips. In tops, I typically wear a regular 14, 12W or L top, although depending on clothing brand, may wear an XL, sometimes even a 14W. On occasion, I find that a regular 12 or even a M will fit me best. Regular size 16 is typically too large for me, and I look like I'm swimming in 16W or 1X tops. My upper arms are on the heavier size, so if a top has tight sleeves, it can present an issue for me. I have an hourglass figure; neither top- or bottom-heavy, proportionate with a defined waist. I like my clothing to be figure-flattering and to enhance my shape, with a classic, tailored fit, not loose/baggy but NEVER tight, pulling or overly clingy. I don't mind pieces that are cut close to the body, but anything that shows lumps or creates a muffin top is out - I hate looking as though I have squeezed myself into a too-small size for vanity's sake. I like my sleeve length slightly on the longer side, about 21" from the shoulder seam, so that the sleeve hits above the wrist bone just at the point where my wrist and hand join (this sleeve length covers my watch when I am wearing one). So how did this top fit? I was surprised to find that BOTH the L and XL tops worked for me! The mesh portion of the top fits almost identically in both sizes - it's generously cut and offers a lot of stretch so that my upper arms did not look sausage-like in either size. I did not find that the sleeves were freakishly long, as some reviewers have commented; while long, they were only an inch or so longer than my preferred sleeve length, so I am not likely to shorten them (I often wear my long sleeved shirts with the sleeves pushed up to about 3/4 length). For both tops, the sleeve length was about the same, only a tiny bit longer in the XL than the L. The real difference between the sizes was in the jersey body of the top. On me, the XL had a slightly - and only slightly - looser fit than shown in the images worn by the model, still shaped, slim fitting and flattering. The L was much more figure-hugging, cut very close to the body; not as close as a leotard, and not tight or pulling, but snug. Both fit me - I agree with reviewers who've stated that the cut of this top is more along the line of juniors' sizing than misses - the torso is narrower than I find with misses sizing. On neither size (both were tried on with and without a bra) did I find the issue with the inside lining across the bust that others have mentioned - despite being a 38DD/40D, I did not have any noticeable line across my bust with this top. It does appear that the extra lining can easily be cut out if needed, as another reviewer has mentioned. The verdict? For my current size, I will most likely opt to wear the XL, as I don't plan to wear this top with a jacket. Perfect for a night out with the girls or date night with my husband, it's cute and stylish, comfortable, I like the shaped fit, and the shoulder seams are where they should be - not halfway down my upper arms, as I find with some other XL tops. That said, I am in the midst of a significant weight loss effort - I've lost more than 50 pounds over the past 14 months. I would expect that by January/February 2016 as my efforts continue (given that we've just entered the holiday eating season!) the L will prove to be a better fit. So I'll keep both! My advice: - If you are a size 16 regular or 14W, choose the XL top. The L will be much too tight across your torso. - If you are a size 14 regular or 12W, choose the XL top if you want a fit slightly looser through the torso (but still shaped) than shown on the model, if you like a snug, body-hugging fit through the torso, choose the L top. - If you are a size 12 regular, choose the L top; you may even want to opt for the M (although this is not one that I ordered). - In general, if you typically purchase junior sizes and are narrower in the torso, go with the L; if you prefer a misses cut and are a bit wider through the torso, choose the XL. - And if you're a Prime member, buy them both with the free returns option and decide for yourself! Hope that this was helpful!

clean_review_title: Made in China. Nice deep black.
clean_review_text: I have the black color. The listing pictures, at least on my computer, make it look almost charcoal. But in person it is indeed a true black. It is made of 67.5% viscose (rayon) 27.5% cotton and 5% spandex. The fabric is soft and smooth. Anytime you see fabric which is counting being made of bamboo, it’s actually rayon. Think of it this way… Paper is made from trees, but it takes a lot of chemical processing to turn a tree into paper. The same applies with bamboo. It takes a lot of chemical processing to term bamboo into rayon. So it’s not really that “environmentally friendly” especially when compared to cotton. I have the size small. It measures 27 inches from the top to the bottom. It measures 19 inches across in the middle. This shirt washes well, but you need to hang it after drying. Otherwise it really wrinkles if you simply fold it. This shirt is made in China and being sold by Chinese company on Amazon. product_title: Metal Wire Rim Rectangular Frame Reading Glasses with Spring Hinge Various Strengths and Colors LARGE SIZE
clean_review_text: I like to "live" with a pair of reading glasses for a few days to see how they fit and feel, as well as making sure the strength works like it should. I had cataract surgery a few years ago, and I'm finding I need different strengths in reading glasses for different activities: working on the computer, reading, driving (reading the dials and gauges), and needlework (thread/lace crochet). I purchased these in the stronger strength (3.0) for my needlework, and so far, they're working fine. I have a rather wide face, and many of the less expensive readers are just too narrow. These fit great -- they're wide enough that there are no pinching or pressure points, and they stay in place on my nose. The strength matches a more expensive pair of 3.0x readers, so I'm confident in the accuracy of these. My only quibble is that they're a bit on the heavy side compared to plastic-frame readers. It's not uncomfortable, but it is noticeable. This is, I'm sure, due to the metal frames plus the thickness of the lenses. It's not a deal-breaker for me, but I thought it deserved mentioning. Overall, I'm really pleased with these. I've been using them daily for about 10 days and I've noticed no deterioration at all -- nothing is loose or feeling "flimsy." I may order another pair in a lower strength to use for reading.

clean_review_text: This is a very attractive stainless steel bracelet in an electric blue. It is designed for men but it works well for women as long as the links are adjusted. It is adjustable in the sense that links can be removed with the link adjuster. That is not an easy task but there are online videos to assist, There are NO instructions provided instructions in the box. The magnets are on the underside of the bracelet in every link. The magnets are supposed to confer health benefits such as pain relief, arthritis relief, body balancing, improve blood pressure, etc. I do not subscribe to such hocus pocus and, after having worn bracelets like this previously and tried this one, I have experienced no health benefit. However, wearing a piece of jewelry that is attractive is certainly mood enhancing. The current price of $18.99 is excellent for this. The only reason I rated this 3 stars is because of the health benefit claims that I do not see; otherwise it is a 5-star bracelet at a good price. If some health benefits are achieved for a select few individuals, then I consider that a bonus. It is definitely a recommended buy as jewelry. NOTE: Anyone with a pacemaker should not wear magnetic bracelets.

clean_review_title: Really Cute Top Especially With Bright Leggings.
clean_review_text: I have this pair of leggings that have photos of Macrons on it- the bright beautiful colored little pastries that come in yellow, pink, aqua, green, yellow, etc. that look like a tiny whoopie pie with filling in it. This shirt is the PERFECT companion to those. Let's take a look at this shirt. WHAT IS THIS SHIRT LIKE? First of all, when I got this shirt, I ordered it because it was advertised as almost all cotton with a little spandex in it. I prefer cotton shirts. When I received it, however, it was made of 82%rayon 11%nylon 7% elastane. It just so happens that I can wear rayon and nylon. If it had been polyester, I would have been more upset about the incorrect labeling. The appearance is like a T-shirt with set in sleeves and light rouching on the bottom sides. The color is a cotton candy pink- nice perfect pink color. HOW DOES THIS SHIRT LOOK? The shirt itself is cute. The sleeves are short but not too long or too short- they hit at the top third of biceps. The hem is straight not angled. The arm holes are on the tight side for a large, but they are not too tight that I can not wear this. I have larger upper arms and this shirt is not too tight at the hems like many are, so I am glad about that. The length is nice- I am about a size 14 and over 5'6" which is a bit tall for females and this fits well. Sizing is about right for a 14. If you are a 16, I am thinking this might be a bit snug for you. WASHING? Rayon has a tendency to shrink, so I would like to mention that this is hand wash and air dry shirt only. The instructions are symbolic, but I seem to feel that they agree with my assessment. No other instructions are given for care. THE BOTTOM LINE For a cute form fitting shirt, this is a nice choice to wear for relaxing. Goes great with leggings or yoga pants. Although it is for action wear, hand wash/air dry is important if you want to keep it from shrinkage. Also it runs a bit small so order a size up if in doubt. MY RATING Five stars. I do like this shirt, the workmanship and fit, but instructions for care should be written more clearly at least on the tag for the US market.

This does hold my hair back, though from preference I would rather have the teeth to help anchor it. and to allow my hair to poof behind it. I see it as jewelry for the hair. As with any headband, the fit will vary. The sturdy metal headband may create pressure over time, or it could be too loose. I have a large head, and am more apt to meet headbands that feel too small. This fits, but I can feel the pressure. I usually wear stuff like this for short periods of time- I like it enough to want to wear it and recommend it. I also have this in turquoise, which I really like against my dark hair. Slightly more classy, where as the turquoise one has a slightly more casual boho vibe to it. I'll include a few pics. product_title: Half Moon Umbrella Base
clean_review_title: It was pretty much as advertised
clean_review_text: It was pretty much as advertised, heavy and solid as you would expect. My issue is that I don't think a half-moon turned out to be a good way to support my patio umbrella on the balcony, wish I had bought a circular base instead now. I hadn't considered this, because I wanted my half umbrella close to the wall, but it turns out that there is moulding near the roof of my condo which doesn't allow it too close. My umbrella blew down twice during a particularly windy day and nearly tore my hammock. I am not blaming this particular brand, of course, but I think that people should only buy the half-moon if there is someway of supporting the half umbrella higher up, such as a Velcro strap ( not an option in my case given how the balcony is arranged ). When it's just a normally windy day, this stand is stable enough though; I just feel like a full circle case would impart a bit more stability on the extremely gusty days.

clean_review_title: I'm happy, and will likely purchase again from sock manufacturer ...
clean_review_text: A recent embrace of style had spurred the realization that my sock collection was rather blasé, consisting mostly of hard-worn gold-toe fluffies and other generic socks. Esteemed sock manufacturer, James Fiallo, rectified my issues by providing this convenient 12 pack assortment of startlingly interesting socks. The patterns are colorful, as indeed claimed, but also tasteful and I've received more complements than snickers (not that it would stop me, anyway) upon donning myself of the aforementioned footwear. There are plenty of colors to match with different colored pants and shoes, several blacks and blues along with many mid-hue green/orange/yellow and several purple colors as well. Socks are an average thickness, but closer to dress socks than athletic socks, not for working out, only for style. They provide just about no warmth, so prepare some warm shoes if you are prone to cold feet (in the empirical, as opposed to metaphorical, sense). My only other complaint is that two of the pairs are exceptionally small, considerably more so than the others, reducing their 'ability-to-be-put-on' status to 'impossible' (for my feet, a solid US 12, at least). I'm happy, and will likely purchase again from sock manufacturer extraordinaire, James Fiallo.

clean_review_text: Unbelievably bad quality, and it looks Nothing like the picture. This might be one of the worst products I've gotten from Vine, and the fact the listing shows $19.99 for something I would pass up at the dollar store is really something. Here's a rundown of why this is junk: Fabric: cheapest kind of chiffon available (I am a costume designer, so I know, and I would never use this fabric for anything), completely sheer (NOT like the photo), and so light that it has no flow. Design: again, not like the picture. The photo shows something long and flowing, with a nice wide hem that tapers to tie on the side (or wherever). This is not cut the way you see in the photo. It is almost a rectangle, with very little at the hem, and therefore no flow. Also, it is not "One Size". It is a small. I am a large (not XL, and not even a bigger large), and even if I wear it WAY too high on my waist, it still gapes open, covering nothing at all. Also, it will be too long on shorter women. I’m 5’4, and I don’t wear heels at the beach, and this drags on me. Not that I’ll be wearing it out. See photo for how high I’m wearing this and the huge gap still there because it’s definitely not one size. Overall: This is not the product in the photo, and it is Very poorly made with junky materials. Do Not Buy. Zero stars if I could.

The pockets are the perfect size to allow credit cards to slip in and out without too much resistance, and the leather is sticky enough to keep them in even if you hold it upside down. The ID holder is big enough that you dont have to remove the ID whenever it needs to be seen, and the pockets behind the card holder and ID holder are big enough to hold a little cash, receipts, or business cards. This is definitely streamlined, as it is only big enough to hold the essentials, but if you dont have much to carry around, this wallet is perfect. My husband has been very pleased with it so far. I did receive this item at a reduced cost and/or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review, but this item is well worth paying full price for, in my opinion. It's a very nice wallet.

Not only did the suit look good, I looked good, and I felt good about myself. The ultimate test for this suit has been our 3 times a week test to the community center were I started taking my son for swimming lessons. The suit has been great to wear with him, it keeps me covered up, and I don't mind be with him in the water. Plus I feel more free to help him and if I needed to go get him I won't be bogged down by heavy wet shorts. I was given this product at a reduce rate for my honest opinion and review. I give this suit 4 out of 5 stars because: Pros: Nice Swim Material Good Stretch Covers the Chest Covers the Thighs Looks cute on Works as intended Not heavy when wet. Cons: Smaller than expected (I wear a large ordered 2x should of gotten a 3x) Shorter dress part then I was expecting even for a short person but I still liked it.

product_title: esstive Women's Soft Velour Midweight Casual Zip-Up Hoodie & Pant Full Set, Black, X-Large
clean_review_title: Fit is too small per stated size
clean_review_text: I got this set through Vine and was not allowed to choose the size. They randomly chose to send me Extra Large. I don't usually purchase sets of clothing which consist of a jacket and pants. This is because I prefer to wear Large on top for a jacket in order to obtain a fit loose enough that it can be worn without constraint over other clothing. If I get the jacket in a size that works for me, then the pants are too big. I normally wear size Medium for pants, whether I purchase women's shorts and long pants or men's elastic-waist, cotton, exercise shorts (which are massively less expensive than the equivalent type of shorts made for women). I thought I would give this set a try anyway, taking a gamble that I might get lucky and both the jacket and the pants might fit me. Technically, by my measurements, I am a size 8 on top and a size 10 on the bottom. Based on that information on sizing, you will probably be as shocked as I was to learn that this supposedly XL jacket is tight enough on me across the shoulders and arms to be somewhat constricting when I am merely wearing a light, cotton t-shirt beneath it. The XL pants, which in any other brand of pants would be falling off of me, are only slightly baggy in the hips and thighs. The elastic waist, which has broad, 2-inch-wide elastic, is only about 6 inches too big at the waist. However, by letting the waistband hang at my hip bones and pulling the drawstring very tight, I can actually manage to keep the pants from falling down. As a result of the poor fit, I wouldn't want to wear this fleece set outside of the house, but inside of the house, they will function sufficiently well for me to wear them as lounging pajamas. If it were possible to order just the pants, instead of a Medium I would order Large. I would not order the jacket at all if that were possible because it is a real problem that an XL does not fit someone whose actual size is Small but prefers to wear size Large. I have not seen for sale anywhere old-fashioned sweatpants in a very long time. They used to be made of a very comfortable, soft, dense, warm, cotton fleece. It seems like every manufacturer of women's workout gear has been trying to get fancy with sweatpants for the past decade and more and, as a result, they are failing to remember that the purpose of sweatpants is to function as comfortable clothing to bend, stretch and vigorously exercise in. These sweatpants are made of a slick-velour fabric that is so relatively lightweight (the manufacturer does admit to it being "midweight" ) compared to what one would expect a sweatpant to be made of that the fabric has an almost sleazy feel to it. Most likely that's due to the fact that instead of being 100% cotton knit, it is 60% cotton and 40% polyester. The jacket has a fairly cheap zipper that I had great difficulty fastening and unfastening, and I don't think it is going to last very long without breaking. I don't like the hood either. Ironically, given how relatively lightweight this velour fleece is compared to old-fashioned sweatpants, the hood feels heavy and unwieldy. When I bend over, the weight of it flopping forward onto my shoulders and head is unpleasant. I washed this set in cold water, on delicate cycle, even though normally, with old-fashioned sweatpants, I would wash them on regular cycle. I also hung them up to dry rather than putting them in the dryer. I did this because the sleazy fabric doesn't seem like it could sustain many washings without wearing out. I also wanted to avoid shrinkage since the clothing being undersized was already an issue. This clothing seems to have survived without damage or noticeable wear and tear after being washed in this careful way.

clean_review_title: The very best cap you can ever get.
clean_review_text: It is an unbiased review of a real customer who paid the full sum to purchase the product and never got any gifts or freebies from the company making the product. It is the second cap that I bought from that company, the first one was lighter one in distressed black for $49, and I loved it. This one is not disappointing as well. First, it is superior quality of the product. The material is natural lamb skin of the highest guality - very soft, silky, pleasant to touch, yet very strong and durable. The stiches are impeccable. Feels very solid and I am sure it will serve for generations to come, not even talking about my life time. In our times of short-living plastic-made chinese knockoffs it is a rarity to find a product tha made of natural materials that is of old-times quality... And for the more-than-resonable price? It feels really warm, but it is not the type of sweaty moist warmish that you get from "polar fleece" synthetic materials. It feels pleasant and dry wamish. The fur is soft and pleasant to touch, not at all itchy to me (a customer who said it was itchy to him, might have been allergic to it, it happens with natural materials). The size... Especially in this cap, because of the thick fur, you have to go one size up. My head circumference is 59 cm, and I ordered the size of 61 cm - I am glad I did, I got ideal fit - not loose at all, and very comfortable. As for the style - it looks very special, almost like cosplay item, and attracts attention, so you decide if that is what you want. I don't have to wear formal and carry suitcase, so this cap is for me. The only con (which I don't care about), you can only wear the hat the way it is. If you try to flip the long side ends to the back or to the top of your head - it looks a kind of ridiculous, so forget it. And it is really warm - I think I should move to Alaska to take the full advantage of this cap. In NJ there will be not too many days that I could wear it.

product_title: HEARTEK Noise Cancelling Headphones Kids Adult Earmuffs Shooting Ear Protection
clean_review_title: they will not comfortably fit an adult
clean_review_text: First and foremost, these are labeled and intended to be children's earmuffs, they will not comfortably fit an adult. I ordered these specifically for my 4.5-year-old daughter who has sensory processing disorder. She does not like loud noises, however, she loves fireworks as long as we are either inside or a distance away. I thought these would be the perfect compromise for her. She can view the fireworks from a closer distance, but the Heartek earmuffs will protect her hearing and make her feel safe and calm simultaneously. We've already tested this theory because one of our neighbors decided to put on a show this past weekend and my daughter loved her new earmuffs! We always help her take them off or put them on though. These have a lot of force and if her hands were to slip during grasping them they would snap together with quite some force. We don't want her to accidentally get hurt so we made the rule that an adult has to help her with them. Overall, these are a great pair of noise reducing earmuffs and my daughter enjoys wearing them. I received this product at a promotional price in exchange for my unbiased feedback. all opinions expressed within this review are solely mine.

product_title: RCA RFR465 4.6-Cu-Ft Refrigerator, Stainless Steel Door, 4.5, Stainless
clean_review_title: Wish I had read here first...
clean_review_text: I didn't want to rate with any stars, because I have a slightly different model, FR464, but I think that refers to the color too.However, I can't leave a review without a star rating. We bought an Igloo 4.9 cu ft fridge in black (FR464) from another website. It is very, very close to this model, so I want to share our Igloo experience. First off, the site had the wrong photos of the insides, so I didn't get the freezer I wanted. While I suppose this was a site issue, it could have also been sent that way from Igloo. I don't know, but I only got a 1/2-width freezer instead of one the full width of the fridge. Pros: Looks great in the room. Plenty of door storage. Reasonably quiet. Cons: Does not keep things very cold in a warm environment. Shelves not very adjustable - you are stuck with a shallow shelf at the bottom. It will hold a 16.9 oz bottle of water on its side, and that's it. Freezer is not self-defrosting. It is very disappointing that a company such as Igloo, who has been in the business of keeping things cold for decades, could not do a better job on their small refrigerators. We have ours cranked all the way to the top setting. It isn't in a hot garage - it is in the upstairs bedroom. It still can't handle warm temps! It has been very warm here this week. While we run the A/C endlessly, this little fridge can't give us a cold drink. All we get are bottles that have a slight, well how should I say this, a clammy feel to them. You can tell they were in a colder environment than the room, but the drinks themselves (bottled water, 24 oz pepsi's and juice boxes) are NOT cold. This has been a major disappointment. I had already thrown the packing material away as it had been doing a pretty good job and I trusted the brand name. Who knew that it was so dependent on the temperature in the room as to whether or not you would get a cold drink?? Seriously disappointing, and it will be my LAST Igloo purchase. UPDATE 08/04/11: Well, we've now had this three months. So far, we've had lots of drinks that were barely colder than room temperature. The freezer door (which was NOT the interior that was pictured with the listing when we bought it - they had the interiors mixed up with a Haier) had so much frost build-up that the door broke completely off a couple weeks ago. No, we didn't use the freezer - it didn't do anything but freeze that little mini tray of ice cubes. The freezer pops (those tubes that look like they are filled with koolaid) don't even freeze in there. I thought hey, maybe with that little freezer section open, the drinks will get colder. Nope! We have it cranked all the way to 7, the highest this will go. The drinks are not cold by any means. It does a nice job of keeping things like cheese or fruit, and not too bad on yogurt. However, I wanted to have COLD drinks this summer, and I am not getting them. We have bottled water and Pepsi in the fridge. Nothing in a plastic bottle gets very cold at all. We also have some cans of ginger ale in the can rack. Because of the aluminum, they are a little bit colder than the bottles, but not a lot. I was putting some more water in there this afternoon, and the inside was so warm and clammy that I checked to see if it stopped working already. I checked the plugs to be sure it was still plugged in, and it was. I turned it off for a few minutes to let it rest, then turned it back on. I did not hear a thing. So, I'll check it tomorrow to see if it died completely. I don't think it did - I just think it cools very poorly and inefficiently. I COMPLETELY regret this purchase. Steer clear of the Igloo models. The other brands are a little more (some places, just $30-$50 more), but you might get something that actually cools your drinks down. This model can't seem to do it. Big disappointment. This is the most expensive thing I've bought online in a long time, and the joke was on me. Shame on Igloo for making something so shabby. It looks great on the outside, but can't do the job you hire it to do. I didn't need another thing taking up floor space - I needed a bedroom refrigerator.

Not a big deal but coil electric cooktops don't make that kind of noise. The 1-9 heating modes are easy enough to use and turning it down is an almost immediate sort of thing like cooking with gas. clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:38225654b62b7bb5f80d41c3eec3b6ef]] UPDATE: I've gotten the machine hooked up to the standard water & drain in my apartment. It took a little work & time at Lowe's, but now everything works great. You have to turn the water off/on where a standard washer you set to hot/cold etc, but it fills up fast so no real problems there. Overall we're glad to have it in our apartment. 3.5 stars. The size is great, machine works very well. Reasons for not giving 5 stars: There is no way to attach a longer drain hose without great difficulty-the hose is glued together inside, behind the back panel. The hose is really too short. The machine is pretty wobbly during use, I imagine due to all of the water & weight being on the left side during use. I tried this out just on my kitchen table with towels underneath to see if/how everything worked, & will definitely be looking for a sturdier stand of some kind. I wish there was a bit more drainage pressure on the wash side, I'm having to do multiple rinses to get out all of the soap. If you get one of these, use very very little soap! I used maybe a teaspoon & a bit of stain release liquid, & it was too much. The box was pretty damaged, but so far I can't tell there was damage to the machine. There really needs to be more protective packaging. However, it did come completely assembled, all I had to do was set it on the table. I didn't attach the water inlet hose for this testing, I just poured in the water, but the hose was included. Overall, I'm glad to have this in my apartment, & will definitely be using it. It washes & spins very well. It has enough power to tangle up any strings on your clothes, so be ready for that. Clothes feel amazingly dry when you get them out of the spinner.

product_title: True TDD-1 Single Door Direct Draw Beer Dispensers
clean_review_text: An update. After one year of use my True Beer Dispenser died. Now runs continuously, will not cool below 48 F. Again, no warranty coverage is provided by True although I paid $1,200+ for it. So this has been a bad purchase experience. I am going to write it off / not pay to get it fixed again. My lesson learned for those interested in a True Beer Dispenser - save your money. Buy a refrigerator (you can buy a new one for less than $600 / much less for a used one), take out the shelves and convert it to a Kegorator. Not only will you have room for two 1/6 kegs + CO2 tank, you will also have additional room for a refrigerator shelf to store stuff, plus in door storage plus a top freezer to chill your glasses. You will also have a more reliable, quieter running product with a warranty and you will save $600+ dollars to stock it. That is what I did!! So learn from my experience. ------------------------------------------------------------------------- I posted the following review 2 months ago. Well, I retract everything below - I was "so" wrong about TRUE's warranty service. I just received a bill for my warranty service with just one sentence from TRUE: "WARRANTY DOES NOT APPLY TO RESIDENTIAL APPLICATION". Now I am being asked to pay $284.36 to repair a defective product. Of course, I was told this AFTER the cooler had been repaired. If I had known I would be paying $1200+ for product WITHOUT a warranty - and then paying $284+ to repair it - I never would never have purchased it. I purposefully paid a premium price to purchase a "made in USA" product - so much for my good intent. There is much better value for your money with other manufacturers who do stand behind their product and provide a warranty. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- I have only had this cooler for approx. 2 months. I researched extensively before purchasing (just ask the bartenders) and this was their recommendation for a 20+ year quality trouble free product. So far I am impressed with both True product quality and True customer service. After setup, the cooler did not keep temperatures as cool as I would prefer. True warranty service was extremely prompt in sending out someone to look at it and improve the thermostat setup for a lower temperature. What has impressed me the most is how quickly I am connected with an experienced True customer service rep when I call and how friendly and helpful True customer service has been. It was a premium price purchase for a premium / commercial quality product. I am glad I spent the extra $$$ for a US product.

clean_review_title: Easy economical fix. Beats using a candle lighter.
clean_review_text: tl;dr - don't procrastinate. Easy economical fix to restoring sparks to burners. If I can do it, you can definitely do it! Have had GE Stove since 1997. Surface burners stopped lighting themselves 10 years ago. Appliance repair was going to cost too much and decided to temporarily use a candle lighter. Watched YouTube videos. Thought about fixing it myself. 5 years ago put this in my cart and then moved it to "Save for Later". Bought at least 4 more candle lighters including the new USB plasma ones that works great. 1 year ago, oven stopped working. Luckily have combination micro/convection oven to bake things in, though tiny compared to full size oven. Microwave was installed too high so had to get on step ladder to safely use it as an oven. 2 months ago, decided to buy stove. Prices going up due to tarriffs. Didn't like price nor thinner sheet metal on newer stoves compared to the tank I bought 20+ years ago. 3 month ago, finally bought this part, "Used-Very Good" from volgacom. Took a gamble since part plus shipping was so much cheaper from volgacom vs new from Amazon with free shepping. 3 days later received good as brand new part. No signs of wear or use. SCORE! Waited 2 more months. Though replacing this thing was going to be complicated. Yesterday, decided to make repair. Didn't have to dismantle burners. as anticipated from YouTube videos. Module is located behind control panel. 8 screws later, I was in. Only two wires to disconnect from old module and reconnect to new module. Snaps in place. Done. Put panel back together, restored power to stove. First try, all burners spark and light as if the stove were brand new. Will update review with longevity of part. Very happy with part and volgacom as the supplier. Like used parts not only saving money but also reducing carbon footprint.

clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:deb7a7b9f478e538d957cb4003f30987]] This is the egg rack that I've been looking for! It stacks vertically so it minimizes its footprint in the fridge, it holds three layers of 10 eggs each. The super cool feature I was not expecting is the shelves that hold the eggs are spring loaded and automatically fold up out of the way when that shelf is empty. The weight of a single egg is enough to keep the shelf deployed downward, as soon as the last egg is lifted it automatically folds up and out of the way to expose the next layer of eggs. It seems to be made slightly too thin plastic, I don't think it's going to last , long. Another issue is mine received a very small crack during shipping, which is not the end of the world except it is in a very vulnerable location, in the very center of the top rack., So structurally it is in the weakest part of the shelf. That's a bad spot because it could easily spread. Perhaps if they made this out of slightly heavier plastic this would not be an issue. Another flaw is that if you stack two eggs directly on top of each other they can touch, this isn't a real big problem just something I noticed.

product_title: Electric Range
clean_review_title: An almost perfect electric range, but with aggravations
clean_review_text: I am a serious home French pastry chef. Five years ago I bought the then-current version of this stove. My main interest was the convection oven. Indeed, this oven is extremely good in even baking. There were many other features I liked, although I found the controls a bit confusing. For guests and first timers, the controls can be VERY confusing. I would still have given it 5 stars. I bought this new version for a different home. There is one change that I find so annoying that I downgraded this new model in my view to 4 stars. This is the temperature readout for the main oven. On most digital readouts, including the earlier version of this stove, you start by setting the target temperature. Then as the oven heats up, it shows the actual temp. This gives you an idea of how much longer you need to wait. If you are cooking something at a non-critical 350 degrees, you might just put it in at 325. Similarly, if you open the oven, say to turn a tray, you know exactly how much the temp has dropped. Not with this new version. You set the target temp, then wait for a long beep to tell you the oven has reached the target temp. There is no way to tell the current temp. While heating up, some lights flash in an incomprehensible sequence. Neither the manual nor Fridigaire customer support could explain what those lights mean. Even with a totally manual oven (like the small side oven here), you can get an idea of current temp by rotating the knob and listening for the thermostat click. Another downgrade was the minimum temp setting on the old oven was 100 degrees, a sometimes useful setting in baking. The minimum on this oven is 150. I wrote to Frigidaire, an actual letter to the director of marketing with the comments above. He never replied. ____________________________ A belated followup: It took a while, but eventually I did get some very helpful responses from Frigidaire customer support and their marketing department. I am very happy with the stove.

product_title: FRIGIDAIRE EFIC189-Silver Compact Ice Maker, 26 lb per Day, Silver (Packaging May Vary)
clean_review_title: Side of the device "melted" and the fan shakes so hard the unit turns off.
clean_review_text: We were super excited about getting the new ice machine and things were going great, until they weren't. Received the ice maker approximately 28 Nov 21 - Did not plug it in and put it into service until approximately 24 Jan 22. Hey, we have some neurodivergence in the house so it took a little bit to get there. This ultimately was the "downfall" for being able to return this product as the return time ran out on 31 Jan 22. The good: This thing pumps out ice at the rate the manual and listing states. It has some cute cubes and they are small enough to fit most of the reusable bottles/cups we use here in the home. The not so good: After 31-days of use the fan (located on the back right side of the unit) seems to be loose or broken and now, at least once a day will rattle so loud and hard that the unit shuts down. There is, what appears to be, a spot where the unit overheated and "melted" the side, to the tune of an approximate area of 6" x 4" as notated by the picture with the ruler. The unit began to "auto shut off" about 2-weeks into use, but it was not consistent and we could not figure out why, shortly after is when we noticed the melted section. So it seems there may be a defect (possibly just with the one we purchased) in this one. Maybe this unit does not support being plugged in 24hrs a day? I may have missed that in the instructions. However, for a $129 unit to last 31 days, just long enough to get past the return point, that also does not seem right. We plan to find a new countertop ice maker, likely from a different company, we expected more from a Frigidaire product. We do hope you have great success with your item if you choose to purchase this product.

product_title: Tier1 DA29-00020B Refrigerator Water Filter | Replacement for Samsung DA29-00020A, HAFCIN/EXP, HAF-CIN, 46-9101, DA97-08006A-B, WSS-2, WF294, Fridge Filter
clean_review_title: Best water filter for our Samsung ever!
clean_review_text: What a great deal!! I know many point out that this doesn't ave the rating to get out all the elements that some filters do, but we're lucking enough that, even though we have well water, our water is pretty good. Even though we're on the Eastern Shore Delaware, and near water, and even have a shallow well, our water is great. Many people compliment the taste (actually the lack of) our water. With that said, we have a 32cf Samsung French Door refrigerator that uses this cartridge. After paying $49 every 6 months, I wanted to find a cheaper solution. I found and purchased these. Since using these filters, I have nothing but positive results. Ice cubes are larger and a glass fills with water faster. The ice cubes are larger because they mechanism fills like it should instead of timing out before filling with water. What used to take over 2 minutes to fill a 32oz glass with water now only takes about 20 seconds. 20 seconds!! This is after 2 service calls on our refrigerator and both ice makers being replaced. I had checked the service line, and replaced the spigot. I removed the fill line many times and flushed the line, and had plenty of pressure before in connects to the solenoid on the back of the unit. After replacing the filter with these, we now have the ability to fill our water glasses FAST.

product_title: Commercial Ice Maker Machine Self Clean, 100lbs/24H Stainless Steel Ice Machine 45 Cubes/Batch in 11-18 Minutes with 33lbs Ice Storage Bin, Freestanding Ice Maker for Restaurant/Home/Food Truck
clean_review_text: I've wanted a built-in ice maker for some time. There are 7 of us sharing the ice maker in a fridge and it just never cut it! I had two options in ice makers: nugget or cube. Nugget ice is much more fun, but it's not good for coolers. Cube ice works for both. So I got the cube maker. If I ever win the lottery, I'll get the other one too! Anyway, the machine was a pain to install, but that's not its fault. First I hired someone because they had to move my fridge, tap a water line, and break through the wall to install the drain. They screwed all of that up and I ended up with a leak, a damaged fridge, and the drain line was leaking as well. They fixed two of the three a week later and now it works well (though the fridge is another story). The instructions were poor, that caused some of the problem (even the professional misread them). I mean, if you're selling a $500 product, can't you afford proper instructions with real pictures written by someone who speaks English? All that aside, there is one thing I took off a star for: The unit doesn't stay frozen! It makes ice and dumps it in a fridge. It melts because that is a fridge not a freezer. And then it makes more ice. This causes a lot of wasted water and electricity, and it means that you absolutely must have that drain line installed! I had another one of these some time back that cost a lot less and made about half as much ice a day. That one was insulated and stayed below 0, so the ice never melted! I didn't even connect the drain line and in 4 years of use, it never leaked. If I were to go back to the start of this project, I would have gotten that again. That's the reason I took off a star. The other style would have saved energy and cost $200 instead of $500... But overall, considering that I got the item at a discount here, I'm okay with this outcome.

clean_review_text: I've had it three days now and done several loads. As everyone else said, the hose will not fit a sink in the US. For the first day, I used bowls to fill the machine which was a lot of work but did the job. The next day, I bought a funnel big enough to cover my shower head, drilled two holes on opposite sides at the top, and used a small bungie cord to put a hook into each hole and then hang it over the shower head. I then cut two slits into the hose to allow the funnel to fit inside the faucet end, then duct taped it to seal. I attached the hose to the machine and it worked perfectly. My shower is low flow so I could turn it on full. I put the machine in the shower and ran it from there with no issues, other than to hold the machine when the spinner starts up because the vibration is very strong. My overall reaction is that it does a good job cleaning and the spinner is very good. Similar to a spa's machine to dry your bathing suit. It is labor intensive because you must move the clothes a few at a time to the spinner and you can only put a few things in at a time, but my clothes dry beautifully on a rack and the towels only need 15 minutes in a dryer at the laundromat until I can string up a clothesline in the summer. I did purchase a warranty, though. I'd rather pay $13 than lose $110 if it breaks.

clean_review_title: Only one lid, so plan to stack these
clean_review_text: I was thinking this came with two lids, allowing for a side-by-side setup, but it only has one. Actually, given the way I'm likely to use these, one lid is really all I need, even if used side by side. I have one Easter Egger hen, Pippin, who really puts out the eggs. Not enough eggs that I don't often buy an 18-count carton at the grocery store, but enough that I have to be concerned about keeping track of which eggs are the oldest so they can be used first. A "first in/first out" setup like you find with can rollers is ideal, and until now I didn't have that option for eggs. My countertop spiral egg skelter has too steep of a roll and, sometimes, one of Pippin's eggs will be too big to fit in it. These egg holders, first and foremost, fit in the fridge very well. They roll the eggs gently. The incline is slight, so the eggs definitely roll down when you take one out, but not so fast that the eggs break. To do a first in/first out storage, however, requires always having to take the lid off to put the new egg(s) toward the back, so I won't use the lid. This is okay with me. I'll use the bottom egg holder for my 18-count white large eggs that I get from the grocery store and the top egg holder (sans lid) to put each day's new egg toward the back of a stack. (Or maybe I'll use the lid with the grocery store eggs and put Pippin's egg holder beside it with no lid. I don't want to have to take the lid off each day I add an egg. This will leave room to put something else on top of the lidded egg tray.) I haven't quite worked out my rotational system yet, but I'll probably plan to pull my eggs from left to right and also put in new eggs from left to right at the top (back) of the stacks. Right now, I've got 10 blue eggs, so I put three stacks of three and then the extra egg at the top of the left stack. As I take out the eggs, the stacks will roll down and the fresher eggs will be in the back. I won't have to remember which ones are which, and this will work great. For the lower holder, I will fill the whole thing with 18 eggs, pull them from the tray until they are gone, and then order a new carton. The piece that fits the lower egg holder for stacking another tray on top is very sturdy and neatly designed. I really like this set of egg holders, and it's definitely going to make my life easier.

product_title: Whirlpool 8300802 Fixed Thermostat for Stove
clean_review_text: This is the infamous Whirlpool/Kitchenaid thermal fuse that blows when you run the cleaning cycle on the Kitchenaid KGSA906PSS range. You know, the problem everyone has, but Kitchenaid denies even happens; the reason I'll not buy Kitchenaid appliances again. All because of this fuse. Or rather, the poor implementation of the fuse into the design. Anyway, when it blows about an hour into the cleaning cycle because by design the oven gets so hot it blows the fuse (good job, Kitchenaid!), the door will remain locked closed and the timer display will go black. No clock, no controls. The burners will still light. That means power is getting to the stove, but the oven will be dead. So if you need the oven, you are out of luck. DO NOT CLEAN THIS OVEN if you need it within a week. You will have to turn off the gas, unplug the power and disconnect the supply pipe from the range (I do that by removing the storage drawer and reaching underneath to disconnect from the range) then pull the range out enough so you can get behind it. There's a 24"x24" panel on the back held on with about 15 black square drive screws and an upper panel that is held on by a few Phillips drive screws. After removing the panels, you can see the fuse, attached to the back of the oven box, waiting for you to run the cleaning cycle so it can blow and cost you $40. After I got the replacement part reinstalled and put all the screws back in, plugged it in and reconnected the gas, everything worked as before. So I went out to the garage and took the blown fuse apart. It's a shame you cannot reset the fuse. There's no real reason you can't except they didn't design a little reset button into the fuse. The fuse "blows" when a little bimetal disc inside the fuse heats up and pops like a bottle cap, which allows a little plastic piston to move. This in turn allows two electrical contacts to separate. This turns off the power to the display, leaves the door locked and shuts off the gas to the stove. The oven then cools down, but the fuse stays in the "off" mode. After replacing the part and getting the oven to work, I broke the Bakelite open on the old fuse to see the innards. All I really needed to do was drill a tiny hole through the metal outer cap (but not through the bimetal) then poke a toothpick into the hole and pop the bimetal disc back into its original shape. It's not worth the trouble, but in a real pinch, it might work. The other way is to jumper over the two contacts, but of course, that's not recommended. We've had this range since 2005, though, and the fuse never blew before...because we never ran the cleaning cycle. So the fuse is there only in case you run the cleaning cycle. Apparently the oven box gets so hot during the normal cleaning cycle by design it can't stand it. Both my wife and I live-chatted with Kitchen reps. They asked if an electrical fuse had popped at the box and other lame excuses, all while denying there was any reported problem. But a quick net search shows many people have had this problem. It's big enough that Amazon carries this one part and "8300802" returns a lot of hits. I'm glad Amazon had the part. It got here in a hurry and it was the right part. Kitchenaid needs to stick with stand mixers and get out of the appliance biz.

It runs better then a 1990's machine, but it's not the 4 towel drying monster that I've read... Two large towels will range in time between 1.5hrs to 2 "for me" in the North-East (70 degree day). Also, a regular full load of laundry will take 1.5 hours, where a 1/2 load will take 45mins. Living in 1 of the largest cities in the US (with 1930's wiring) the 110V / 1400W (translates to 12.72 amps) hasn't caused a fire in "my apartment." At the end of the day, I don't think that I can find a better product that will work with my electrical constraints, so although my expectations are tempered... I like this machine. FYI: I need my machine to be very portable, so I put it on a 11.5" x 18" mini-dolly. Although it barely fits, the smooth legs do slip off, so I needed to lock the dryer's mini legs in place. I did this by screwing down round mini tins to the dolly's corners, and wrapped velcro (2 sided, no tape) around the machine and dolly. I used the soft side of the velcro against the machine (prevents scratching), but I also used a little tape-down scratchy pieces on top of the machine pointing up. This way the velcro not-only-sticks-to-itself, but it also doesn't shift aroound on top of the machine.... Finally, I cut a piece of Yoga-mat a little larger then the dolly, and place it under the machine to prevent dryer movement during use...

clean_review_text: I really like coffee but I am a huge tea drinker and love to take tea with me when I am on the go but sometimes it can be a pain to brew up a cup of my loose leaf when I need to get going in the morning. Now that I have discovered this however, it has changed things for me. I can totally put in my loose leaf tea and brew it from my Keurig. I know there are a lot of different reusable K-cup things out on the market and while many of them are great (which I own quite a few) sometimes you want a little more filtration and that is where these comes in. The filters that come with these are great and really make for a nice cup of tea, without any of the fine residue that can come from loose leaf tea but it also makes for a great smooth cup of coffee too. Either way you go, you can't go wrong with these. I keep them in my tea box for whenever I want to make a quick cup of tea for the road and the cool part is that I have even reused the filters (for the same type of tea, of course) and they can work again and again for at least to help brew up two to four more cups, at least. They might not recommend doing that, but I like to get every cent out of something and I can even help the environment too by using these, love them. Now the cups on the other hand aren't my favorite at all. It's just like a k-cup, thin plastic. The first time you use it the Keurig will make one hole on the top and one on the bottom of the cup and in order to use it again you can either pop it in and not worry about the other hole or line it up perfectly so that no other holes are made. Yeah, to much work for a cup of tea, for me. While they work just like a k-cup, I didn't reuse them like I did the filters. I actually used the filters in a reusable k-cup that I already had which, for me, worked much better than trying to use these plastic cups. I would totally buy this for the filters but not for the cups.

product_title: Air King AK100L Exhaust Fan, Clear
clean_review_text: Pretty disappointed with this purchase. Spent a lot of time reading reviews. There are a lot of 3 star exhaust fans out there and this was one of the few that received 4+. Was disappointed the moment it arrived. Everything about it is chincy. All 4 metal brackets were bent. It was a breeze to bend them back by hand because the metal was so soft (mind you, these are supposed to be the "sturdy" brackets to mount the unit to your joist). The internal fan unit was somehow installed crooked in the housing when I pulled it from the box? It was like somebody sat on it, causing it to be pushed into the housing on a 45 degree angle...bending some of the internal parts. Had to pull it out and rebend to get it to sit flush. The instructions show 3 screws holding the motor to the fan...but there was only 1 screw. And it's not as though they just forgot some other screws...there weren't even other holes for the other screws! Despite all of these things, I still brought it up to the attic to install. Once I began the electrical work, I just couldn't take it. The box is SO tiny, it is impossible to fit all of your connections in there...even if you had a 12/2 and 12/3! Needless to say, I returned it, spent the extra $95 for a Panasonic and couldn't be happier. The second I pulled the Panasonic from the box, I knew it was money well-spent. Solid, straight, oversized electrical box. Unfortunately, this Air King model just didn't cut it. Actually, it didn't even come close.

clean_review_text: When I was telling a friend about this product, and how well it worked even without using a paper filter, she got so excited and asked if she could bring it to her college dorm in the fall. Limited space, no buying filters, and a really delicious way to brew coffee makes this perfect for her. My only concern with this product, is that I strongly suggest hand washing. That is no problem because all you do is gently tap it with your hand to remove most of the grounds, and then Swish and some soapy water for a few seconds. The reason I say this is because I have a similar one and noticed that for some reviewers, the cone separated from the base, although that did not happen with me. After using this model, I don't think that's an issue as it seems to be very secure. Perhaps I'm being overly cautious by not putting it in the dishwasher, but it's so nice and convenient that it's worth a few extra seconds to clean it. If you are giving this as a gift, please know that it comes nicely packaged. After my friend got so excited, this would be a terrific part of a coffee gift basket for someone going off to college or getting their first apartment. It's also a nice option for someone that doesn't drink coffee, but occasionally entertains people who want just a cup or two, because it doesn't take up much storage space and you don't have to worry about those filters. I received this product in order to test with the option to review. All opinions are my own. After 6 months most items are donated or destroyed. clean_review_title: oh so simple to get clean clothes!
clean_review_text: our apartment building has washers and dryers on property but! they cost money, they're extremely inefficient costing MORE money, they're constantly broken down or broken into, and the landlord locks the doors into the laundry room at random times, sometimes keeping my wet laundry hostage! i've washed my laundry before in the tub and in a galvanized bucket but the effort was just too much. i already work a lot of hours so we just kept handing over stacks of quarters. however, i happened to run across this little guy when i did a random "aesthetic" search of "vintage laundry" and bought it immediately! and after a bit of fiddling, figuring out ratios and jerry rigging a hose from my sink into the washer, its fantastic! you don't need very much soap at all for the size of the load it holds, and in 3 minutes, i got grey water out of the spout after the first wash, and clear water after the second 3 minute run with just water. so in six minutes, i was able to clean all my work pants. another six minutes, and my tops were clean too! i figured that in about 15 or so weeks, it will have paid for what I would have to spend in my apartment's laundry rooms. the washer drum itself was fabulous, i have some mild concerns about the base. it's quite flimsy, wobbling here and there and the suction cups didn't hold on my countertop making me hold it at kind of an awkward angle to make sure it didn't slip off the edge. however, i sat down with it on a rug and it didn't move around, just wobbled. honestly, while thats kinda meh, it is just thin plastic and if those break, i have no qualms with remaking them out of a wood or some such, maybe with an actual handle on the front to steady it. but it spins just fine otherwise as long as you're careful. and you should be careful! spinning it too hard would make the motion useless! going nice and slow creates the sloshing motion you want to get the water through the clothes. all in all, it took me no time at all, even with experimenting, to get my work clothes clean for the upcoming week. i love this little thing!

product_title: Frigidaire 316436001 Switch, Single Unit
clean_review_text: This was ordered as a replacement for a switch that caused the 8-inch element to turn red-hot. The Magic Chef has been used for 30 years. I was not sure this switch would be a satisfactory replacement. It appeared to have the correct number of wiring contacts, and the current it could handle was the higher amount - 8.9 - 11 amperes. The length of the shaft is about an eighth of an inch shorter than my original, and the entire switch was mounted sideways so OFF would be on top, just as it is on all the three other original switches. I used my laser temperature 'gun' to determine that the LO was the lower temperature and the HI was the higher temperature. Other than those two points, the switch is a satisfactory replacement for the 30-plus year-old switch which controls the large eight-inch burner. The indicators on the replacement (Frigidaire) switch - L1, L2, H1, H2 and P - all coincide with the indicators on the original (Magic Chef). If I remember in the future, I'll update on whether this switch lasted any length of time. UPDATE: Middle of August 2017 and the switch is still functioning as it did when it was first installed. This switch rotates easier than the 30-year-old switches do.

clean_review_text: I've used portable dishwashers before and they are AWESOME for people who travel or have dietary restrictions (Kosher, for example, need separate washers for milk and meat dishes). The good news is, this actually seems like it does a decent job and even comes with some complimentary detergent and jet dry in the box, so you can get up and running right away... in some cases, at least. I say that because depending on how you hook it up, you may need additional hardware to connect to your water lines and/or sink. Here's my problem with it- they claim it is a "countertop" style washer, but the thing is actually too big for my counter! I don't have enough clearance under the cabinets to fit, and it has a lot of hoses and tubing to connect as well. I was considering drilling into my counter to feed the tubes to the water supply and drain, but decided to try connecting to the sink instead when we saw it wasn't fitting well under the cabinets. But then it is too close to the sink and with the hoses etc connected, it looks like it is taking over the kitchen more than just an appliance on the counter like a toaster. I've seen smaller counter-top dishwashers that would easily fit better in our space. Perhaps this one does a better job, but at this point I feel like it is too big to fit the way I intended to use it, but too small to be one we install permanently in the cabinet space. Perhaps someone else will find this size useful, I'm a little disappointed because I was so excited for this to work.

product_title: Whirlpool 786324 Igniter
clean_review_title: A fraction of the repair cost compared to calling an appliance guy!
clean_review_text: We have an old Caloric brand wall oven that came with the house. I have no idea how old it is, but within a year or two of living here, the oven stopped working. It would glow, but no gas would come out and it would never start. I called an appliance repair man who determined it was a bad ignitor. He explained to me that the gas won't turn on until the ignitor reaches a certain temp, so either the ignitor wasn't getting hot enough, or it wasn't sending the signal to turn on the gas when it did. Either way, the problem was entirely with the ignitor. He replaced it, and something like $200 later, the oven was back to working. Until it stopped working again about a year after that. This time it didn't even glow, the oven would just do nothing. Instead of another expensive repair, I decided on a hunch to assume the part the repair man brought was lower quality than the original. I looked it up online and found a match here on Amazon. I felt confident that I could swap it myself now that I saw the repair guy do it, and sure enough this did the trick for a fraction of what I would pay him to come back! Its been a little over a month since putting it in, and so far so good. I'm hoping this will last longer than the last one, but even if it doesn't, worst case scenario is that I'll spend $20-30 on an annual repair until we can afford to replace the whole oven! I can deal with that.

product_title: 12 inch Electric Ceramic Cooktop, 2 Rings Radiant Stove Countertop Burners Smooth Top Black Glass Hot Plate for Cooking, No Plug Hardwire 220-240V 3KW
clean_review_title: I lived on a houseboat for 3 years and used both radiant and induction "twosies"
clean_review_text: Houseboats - boats in general - require different setups. Much like RVs, actually. But the thing about that kind of living is that kitchen areas are small and require that things be moveable/storeable. To get to the point, I cooked on both radiant and induction plates with two burners each so I did have a full-size stovetop, but they were easier to store when I needed the countertop for other things. Of the two cooking surfaces, I did find radiant cooktops to be a bit more versatile as induction requires that the utensil have a metal bottom so that the heat reaction would occur. Not that induction is bad, but it does require more thought in the preparation. Radiant heat cooktops are the better part of electric cooking - no coils that are exposed, a flat surface that stays that way (I have had a couple of stove tops that one coiled burner was slightly bent up) and is easy to clean. This surface was fairly easy to install - the electrician was here for right at 30 minutes as I had already removed my traditional oven/stove and replaced it with a Goodwill lower kitchen cabinet. The back of the cabinet was removed, the top I had already cut to fit this drop in unit and that's exactly what I did and then let the electrician do his part to get this wired. Now, if you are wondering what I will be doing without an oven that went out the door with the traditional stove, I have a multi-use full size microwave that also air fries, bakes, and broils. I can fit two full size cookie sheets in my microwave unit and yes, I can cook with metal when using air fry, bake or broil. Back to the radiant heat cooktop - electrician gone, I had been waiting to try it out and grabbed a smaller skillet, a frozen burger and got to it! Radiant heat doesn't cook faster or better if that's what you think, but it works great and my burger was finished in a short time. The burner does take time to cool down, but that's more or less a given with most electric heating. That is probably the biggest plus of induction over radiant cooking, in how it cools down; however, I am more than pleased with how this radiant heat cooktop is truly and completely flat. No knobs to turn/twist! The four burners have a touch-sensitive section in the front to pick which one you want to use and also, two are 1200 watt and two are 1800 watt so you have higher power for stir frying or the like. One last thing - as I mentioned, I had an electrician wire this cooktop because it had to be tied to a 220 breaker/30 amp load in my home's circuit box. Being that I'm not comfortable working with electric lines, best to pay someone who knows what they are doing, right? So, bottom line is, if you are ready to upgrade your kitchen, this radiant heat flat cooktop would be a wonderful addition to your kitchen.

clean_review_title: Powerful, looks great, easy to clean
clean_review_text: This range is great. It's super powerful, I used the center burner for the first time the other day and it cooked things in the wok perfectly (and fast!). I like that there are different sized burners. I usually cook for one, and a small pot will boil over on simmer on all but the smallest burner. Just move it to that one if you're having issues with it being too hot. It looks great, the grates are nice quality and flat, plus they sort of lock onto the bottom so they're all lined up nicely. I like that underneath the burners is all one surface, so you're not scooping out around each burner all the time to clean it. Simply take the grates off and you can wipe the entire bottom down if you want to, including swiping pieces off the side to be able to get them out. There are 2 annoying things. First that the ignition continues for a long time after the flame is on, it takes some getting used to to operate the knobs..also that it won't go past high if you're still pushing on the knob. I'm used to it after a few weeks now though. The other thing is the surface the knobs are on looks dirty if you wipe it with a sponge, you have to get a cloth or paper towel to clean it. I didn't realize that the knobs had so many indentations either, those are not the easiest to clean. I had it professionally installed, so I don't know how easy it was.

clean_review_text: These filters were purchased for our Samsung refrigerator that requires the DA29-00020B filter. The first filter of the 3 pack we just bought started leaking 8 days after installation, flooding the lower refrigerator and started filling up the bottom door freezer. Luckily for us we noticed the water running down into the freezer it before it started to spill out into the kitchen. We installed the 2nd filter hoping the first was just a defective part and just replaced the 2nd filter this evening, 5 days later. It was leaking just like the first filter, not massive amounts but a slow leak that unchecked would have flooded the whole refrigerator again. We have installed the 3rd filter which is holding for now but have also ordered Samsung OEM replacements and will not try "compatible" replacements ever again. It's very apparent when the comparing the mating features of the OEM filter and this replacement, there's a enough of a difference that makes this filter a less than ideal replacement for the DA29-00020B. If they work for you, great, but for our Samsung refrigerator, it was a waste of money.

product_title: Bosch SHE3AR55UC Ascenta 24" Stainless Steel Full Console Dishwasher - Energy Star
clean_review_title: If I could, I would get something else
clean_review_text: Had to replace the dishwasher in a new house. Looked into many models, and decided to go with this model (bought at a local BestBuy). Used for 5 months prior to writing this review. Pros: 1. Washes dishes well. 2. Supposedly saves water (not sure if I can check this claim, so I am taking it as is) 3. Supposedly saves energy cause it's not using dry-heat at the end of the cycle but rather FINISH rinse agent (more on it in #3 of Cons) 4. Nice looks and easy to operate. Mainly use the AUTO wash - set it and forget it. Also we use 3hr delay so we don't get bothered with noise (later about the noise). Cons: 1. It is much smaller than other dishwashers we have owned and/or operated (primarily Kenmoore and Whirlpool). I think this Bosch's basic model was made smaller on purpose to make you buy a more advanced washer that is sized properly. For a family of 3, we turn the dishwasher on at least every other day, sometomes daily (when we have guests and such) 2. Maybe I got a defective dishwasher, but mine is NOT QUITE. Again, I compare with kenmoores and whirlpools that I have used and/or owned. We set the 3-hr delay so that in the evening we can watch the TV in the living room without having to turn the volume up. 3. This dishwasher goes though FINISH [[ASIN:B0026WRHK6 Finish Jet-dry Rinse Agent, Original Liquid, 32 Oz]] lightning fast. We set the use of FINISH to the lowest, but the LOW indicator lights up every three or so weeks. Maybe the dishwasher is saving water (see #2 in pros above) and energy (see #3 in pros above), but last time I checked water is much cheaper than FINISH liquid. With Kenmoore, we had to refill the liquid no often than once every 3-4 months. All things considered, we aren't getting anymore BOSCHs. Next time around I am getting a Kenmoore from Sears. They all are regular sizes and don't consume FINISH at lightning speeds.

clean_review_text: Pros: The pitcher is nicely shaped and does work for steaming as described-- by pointing the steam wand at the bottom of the pitcher. Cons: Kinda pricey understates it, no? It's really a shortcut that, in the end, though stylish and handsome, is not necessary. Practice makes perfect. Items like this and steaming thermometers are aimed at new recruits in the coffee army who become frustrated during boot camp and finally want something that just works. Believe me, I understand, but immerse yourself in videos, online advice, and visit good coffee shops (no, not *$s!). Watch the baristas. Some shops offer classes. While it might seem silly to take a class in "milk chemistry" or such, it's not. You'll gain by watching, listening, and speaking with others trying to master the craft. If you love the look of this pitcher, and I don't blame you if you do-- it's cool looking, for sure-- buy it. But if it's just that you're not having luck steaming milk with a new machine, keep working. Avoid gimmicks that promise you a no effort shortcut. In the end, you'll be a better barista if you just stick with it, get the advice and instruction you need, and finally (and you will) master producing creamy microfoam. Cheers, mates.

product_title: WonderWash Portable Washing Machine for Apartment & Tiny Spaces - Manual Hand Clothes Washer with Retro Design - Clean Laundry Anywhere with Our Countertop, Non-Electric, Small Washer - Blue
clean_review_title: Try a bucket and a plunger, first
clean_review_text: Was excited to get this product. After doing my first two loads, I'm honestly underwhelmed. Got it together, no problem. Put it on a table outside, so I could grab the gray water in a bucket and water the plants. I put in water, soap (which I measured according to their instructions), a little baking soda because of our hard water, and a 2 lb load of light pajamas and undies. Spun it at the right speed -- and heard flop, flop, flop, instead of splash, splash, splash. The lump of wet clothes doesn't mix at all, it just flops from one side to the other in a clump. Ok. Maybe that's ok. Released the lid, swished with my hand for a minute, then put in the drain pipe. Water spilled onto the table. Glad it had a plastic cloth on it. Drained into the bucket. Refilled, added some vinegar, did the whole thing again to rinse. Rinsed a second time. Still bubbly water. Stood there with the hose running into the pot while it drained at the same time, swishing with my hand. Finally ran clear. Took the pieces one at a time and ran them through my salad spinner to get rid of a lot of the water, then hung to dry. The clothes look clean, except for one major problem. We have lots of animals, which means lots of fur. Fur gets removed in the regular washer and dryer. It does not get removed with this. I may try making "washer rings" out of some of plastic needlepoint canvas, and see if tossing some of those in to break up the lump of laundry will help. Or try using dryer balls. Otherwise, I think this will go back and I'll get one of the plunger types and a 5 gallon bucket. Or forget the whole thing and go back to the regular washer.

product_title: Keurig My K-Cup Universal Reusable K-Cup Pod Coffee Filter, Compatible with Keurig Classic/1.0 K-Cup Pod Coffee Makers, 1 Count, White
clean_review_text: I ordered two of these to replace a couple of older ones because the lids cracked in half (see photo). I've had four or five of these lids break along the left side, up to the red disc. Happens every time after a few months of use. Anyway, I haven't had the new ones long enough to see if they too will break, but I'm not a fan of the redesign. The new design has a wire basket with a lid, like the old one, but the basket then goes inside a plastic shell with a single hole in the bottom. So preparing one for use requires two steps instead of one, and cleaning is a pain. The old basket had a "handle" that I used to rap on the edge of a bowl to shake the grounds loose (I save my grounds for gardening). The new basket lacks the handle and is generally sort of flimsy, making it difficult to empty. Also, the new design allows you to make either a cup or a carafe. Okay, not a bad idea, except that now you have to go through another menu, in which you must select either a picture of a carafe or a number, 1-6; then you get to another screen where you can select the size. There's really no explanation of what the numbers mean (I know, it's probably in the Keurig manual somewhere) so we just guess and then see what we get. The whole thing is annoying. Amazon is famous for reducing transactional friction; it's disappointing when a company goes out of its way to increase friction. On the plus side, a cup of coffee seems to brew more quickly. Haven't actually timed the two models, but it feels faster. Regarding Keurig customer service: I recently posted a question about the cracking lids, and someone from Keurig suggested that I call customer support. I did, and it was a waste of time. The rep took all my personal info down and said he would pass it on to someone else who might be able to do something about replacing the broken lids. Or not, he wasn't sure. That was the last I've heard from them.

clean_review_text: One of my kids has issues with sinuses and if the air is too dry, she tends to wake up stuffy and with a dry throat. A humidifier is the key to solving this problem and so I turned to this humidifier from Vycol for the relief my daughter needed. With this humidifier, you get digital accuracy and control. This is one of its best features. You can set it for the exact level of humidity you prefer. It will then keep the air at that level of humidity. It has a timer, too, which is good for my daughter. She can make sure it is off when she is not in the room. Another thing I like about this humidifier is that you can visibly see the mist. With others I have used, the amount of moisture added to the air is too slight to visibly see it. With this humidifier, you can see the moisture shooting out from the top. And for a small bedroom, it adds moisture to the air quickly. You need to be careful not to set it too high for that reason. It adds humidity to a room rather quickly and it doesn’t make much noise. A little moisture in the air is necessary for some people. This table top humidifier from Vycol certainly does its job, as promised. You can control it and set it the way you like. It adds moisture to a room quickly and efficiently and it looks better than other humidifiers. It earns an enthusiastic recommendation from me.

product_title: Frigidaire EFIC452-SSRED XL Maker, Makes 40 Lbs. of Clear Square Ice Cubes A Day, Stainless, Red Steel
clean_review_text: It took a bit of trial and error, but, I got the machine working well. Instructions could have been a lot clearer. Water is poured into a reservoir that holds about 1 1/2 liters. The machine produces a "rack" of ice about every 15 minutes. Four cycles will fill the catch box holding the ice. This needs to be emptied after 20 minutes or so since the ice will begin to melt as if it were in a refrigerator. The melt drains off and recycles through the machine so it is not a problem if one doesn't mind smaller ice cubes; they are small to begin with. The machine works by water flowing from a horizontal tube with many very small holes that allow water to flow over the ice-making rack. It is obvious that the water put into the reservoir must be scrupulously clean. I currently use only filtered water. I think this is essential to prevent a breakdown. The size of the cubes does vary and can be adjusted. Some people put the fresh ice into bags before putting the ice in a freezer. This is helpful since the fresh ice will "clot" and need to be separated somewhat. I use a wood mallet for the purpose. If the ice is placed into a plastic bag just dropping it on a counter should work to separate the ice. The making of ice is quite noisy. If I am in the same room as the machine, it sounds like a truck going by. I am impressed so far. Fridgedaire put a lot of thought into the machine. Cheaper versions of ice-makers have a high rate of negative reviews while Fridigaire has good reviews overall. You will pay for that— It's worth it.
